tEn: Mamba: Linear-Time Sequence Modeling with Selective State Spaces
tZh: Mamba：具有选择性状态空间的线性时间序列建模
sEn: Mamba achieves linear-time sequence modeling with selective state spaces, outperforming Transformers in efficiency and matching larger models in performance.
sZh: Mamba通过选择性状态空间实现线性时间序列建模，在效率上超越Transformer，在性能上匹配更大规模的模型。
absEn: Foundation models, now powering most of the exciting applications in deep learning, are almost universally based on the Transformer architecture and its core attention module. Many subquadratic-time architectures such as linear attention, gated convolution and recurrent models, and structured state space models (SSMs) have been developed to address Transformers' computational inefficiency on long sequences, but they have not performed as well as attention on important modalities such as language. We identify that a key weakness of such models is their inability to perform content-based reasoning, and make several improvements. First, simply letting the SSM parameters be functions of the input addresses their weakness with discrete modalities, allowing the model to selectively propagate or forget information along the sequence length dimension depending on the current token. Second, even though this change prevents the use of efficient convolutions, we design a hardware-aware parallel algorithm in recurrent mode. We integrate these selective SSMs into a simplified end-to-end neural network architecture without attention or even MLP blocks (Mamba). Mamba enjoys fast inference (5$\times$ higher throughput than Transformers) and linear scaling in sequence length, and its performance improves on real data up to million-length sequences. As a general sequence model backbone, Mamba achieves state-of-the-art performance across several modalities such as language, audio, and genomics. On language modeling, our Mamba-3B model outperforms Transformers of the same size and matches Transformers twice its size, both in pretraining and downstream evaluation.
absZh: 基础模型如今驱动着深度学习中最激动人心的应用，几乎普遍基于Transformer架构及其核心注意力模块。为了解决Transformer在长序列上的计算低效问题，人们开发了许多次二次时间架构，如线性注意力、门控卷积和循环模型以及结构化状态空间模型（SSM），但这些模型在语言等重要模态上的表现不如注意力机制。我们发现这类模型的一个关键弱点在于无法进行基于内容的推理，并做出了若干改进。首先，让SSM参数成为输入的函数，解决了其在离散模态上的弱点，使得模型能够根据当前token沿序列长度维度选择性地传播或遗忘信息。其次，尽管这一改变阻止了高效卷积的使用，我们设计了一种硬件感知的并行算法，以循环模式运行。我们将这些选择性SSM集成到一个简化的端到端神经网络架构中，无需注意力甚至MLP块（Mamba）。Mamba具有快速推理（吞吐量比Transformer高5倍）和序列长度线性扩展的特点，在长达百万长度的真实数据上性能提升。作为通用序列模型骨干，Mamba在语言、音频和基因组学等多种模态上取得了最先进的性能。在语言建模方面，我们的Mamba-3B模型在预训练和下游评估中均优于相同大小的Transformer，并匹配两倍大小的Transformer。

contrib.en: Proposes Mamba, a selective state space model that outperforms Transformers on long sequences.
contrib.zh: 提出Mamba，一种选择性状态空间模型，在长序列上优于Transformer。

contrib.en: Identifies content-based reasoning as key weakness of prior SSMs and fixes it with input-dependent parameters.
contrib.zh: 指出先前SSM无法进行基于内容的推理，并通过输入依赖参数解决。

contrib.en: Designs a hardware-aware parallel algorithm for efficient recurrent mode inference.
contrib.zh: 设计了一种硬件感知的并行算法，实现高效的循环模式推理。

contrib.en: Achieves 5x higher throughput than Transformers and linear scaling in sequence length.
contrib.zh: 实现比Transformer高5倍的吞吐量，序列长度线性缩放。

contrib.en: Mamba-3B matches Transformers twice its size in pretraining and downstream tasks.
contrib.zh: Mamba-3B在预训练和下游任务中匹配两倍大小的Transformer。

contrib.en: State-of-the-art across language, audio, and genomics modalities.
contrib.zh: 在语言、音频和基因组学等多种模态上达到最先进水平。

limits.en: Selective SSMs lose convolution efficiency, requiring custom hardware-aware implementation.
limits.zh: 选择性SSM失去卷积效率，需要定制硬件感知实现。

limits.en: Performance on very short sequences may not surpass Transformers due to overhead.
limits.zh: 由于开销，在极短序列上性能可能不如Transformer。

limits.en: Theoretical understanding of why selectivity improves performance is limited.
limits.zh: 对选择性为何提升性能的理论理解有限。

limits.en: Scaling to extremely large models (e.g., 100B+) is not demonstrated.
limits.zh: 未展示扩展到极大模型（如100B+）的效果。

limits.en: Potential numerical stability issues with recurrent mode on long sequences.
limits.zh: 长序列循环模式可能存在数值稳定性问题。
